questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: ______ Eiffel Tower is in Paris.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' before names of well-known buildings and monuments.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: He received ______ MBA from a top university.
questions.explanation: 'MBA' is pronounced starting with the vowel sound /e/ ('em-bee-ay'), so we use 'an'.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: ______ honesty is the best policy.
questions.explanation: No article is used before abstract nouns used in a general sense. 'Honesty' here refers to the concept in general.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: She bought ______ one-rupee stamp.
questions.explanation: 'One' starts with the consonant sound /w/ ('wun'), so we use 'a', not 'an'.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: ______ Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean in the world.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' before names of oceans, seas, and large water bodies.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: My father reads ______ Times of India every morning.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' before names of newspapers.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: He is ______ MP from Chennai.
questions.explanation: 'MP' is pronounced starting with the vowel sound /e/ ('em-pee'), so we use 'an'.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: ______ gold found in this mine is of high quality.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' when a noun is made specific by a phrase that follows it ('found in this mine' specifies which gold).

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: She plays ______ violin beautifully.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' before names of musical instruments when talking about playing them.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: ______ United Kingdom consists of four countries.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' before country names that include words like United, Republic, Kingdom, or States.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: We celebrate ______ Republic Day on the sixth of September.
questions.explanation: No article is used before names of festivals and national days.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: He was born on ______ sixth of September.
questions.explanation: When a date is written in words with 'of' (the sixth of September), we use 'the' before the ordinal number.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: ______ Himalayas stretch across five countries.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' before names of mountain ranges (the Himalayas, the Alps, the Andes).

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: She goes to ______ church every Sunday.
questions.explanation: No article is used before places of worship, meals, or institutions when used for their primary purpose (going to church to pray).

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: ______ UNESCO is a branch of the United Nations.
questions.explanation: 'UNESCO' is an acronym pronounced as a word (/junesko/), not letter by letter. No article is needed before it as it functions like a proper noun.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: He gave me ______ useful piece of advice.
questions.explanation: 'Useful' starts with the consonant sound /j/ ('yoo-sful'), so we use 'a', not 'an'.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: ______ Earth revolves around ______ Sun.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' before unique objects in nature. There is only one Earth and one Sun.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: My sister is learning ______ French.
questions.explanation: No article is used before names of languages.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: ______ poor need our help.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' before an adjective to refer to a class of people. 'The poor' means poor people in general.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: She had ______ hour-long meeting with her boss.
questions.explanation: 'Hour' starts with a silent 'h', so the first sound is the vowel /a/. We use 'an' before silent-h words.

questions.question: Correct the article error: He is a honest person.
questions.explanation: 'Honest' starts with a silent 'h', so the first sound is the vowel /o/. We use 'an' before silent-h words.

questions.question: Correct the article error: She is an university professor.
questions.explanation: 'University' starts with the consonant sound /j/ ('yoo-ni-ver-si-tee'), so we use 'a', not 'an'.

questions.question: Correct the article error: I saw a owl sitting on the branch.
questions.answer: I saw an owl sitting on the branch.
questions.explanation: 'Owl' starts with the vowel sound /a/, so we use 'an', not 'a'.

questions.question: Correct the article error: The Mount Everest is the highest peak in the world.
questions.answer: Mount Everest is the highest peak in the world.
questions.explanation: No article is used before names of individual mountain peaks. We use 'the' before mountain ranges (the Himalayas) but not single peaks.

questions.question: Correct the article error: He bought a umbrella because it was raining.
questions.answer: He bought an umbrella because it was raining.
questions.explanation: 'Umbrella' starts with the vowel sound /uh/, so we use 'an', not 'a'.

questions.question: Correct the article error: I have an BBC documentary to watch tonight.
questions.answer: I have a BBC documentary to watch tonight.
questions.explanation: 'BBC' is pronounced starting with the consonant sound /b/ ('bee-bee-see'), so we use 'a', not 'an'.

questions.question: Correct the article error: The lunch is served at 1 PM in our school.
questions.answer: Lunch is served at 1 PM in our school.
questions.explanation: No article is used before names of meals (breakfast, lunch, dinner) when used in a general sense.

questions.question: Correct the article error: She wants to visit the Japan next summer.
questions.answer: She wants to visit Japan next summer.
questions.explanation: No article is used before names of countries, unless the name includes words like United, Republic, or Kingdom.

questions.question: Correct the article error: I need a information about the new course.
questions.answer: I need information about the new course.
questions.explanation: 'Information' is an uncountable noun. We do not use 'a' or 'an' before uncountable nouns. Use 'some information' or 'a piece of information' instead.

questions.question: Correct the article error: Her car is parked in a front of the building.
questions.answer: Her car is parked in the front of the building.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' (not 'a') in the fixed expression 'in the front of' when referring to a specific location.

questions.question: Choose the correct option: She is ______ honest woman.
questions.explanation: 'Honest' starts with a silent 'h', so the first sound is the vowel /o/. We use 'an' before words with silent 'h'.

questions.question: Choose the correct option: ______ Andaman and Nicobar Islands are in the Bay of Bengal.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' before names of island groups (the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, the Maldives).

questions.question: Choose the correct option: He wants to become ______ UFO researcher.
questions.explanation: 'UFO' is pronounced starting with the consonant sound /j/ ('yoo-eff-oh'), so we use 'a', not 'an'.

questions.question: Choose the correct option: ______ rich should help ______ poor.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' before adjectives used as nouns to refer to a class of people. 'The rich' and 'the poor' mean rich people and poor people in general.

questions.question: Choose the correct option: My brother plays ______ cricket every evening.
questions.explanation: No article is used before names of sports and games.

questions.question: Choose the correct option: She gave me ______ one-dollar note.
questions.explanation: 'One' starts with the consonant sound /w/ ('wun'), so we use 'a', not 'an'.

questions.question: Choose the correct option: ______ Sahara is the largest hot desert in the world.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' before names of deserts (the Sahara, the Thar, the Gobi).

questions.question: Choose the correct option: He has his lunch at ______ school.
questions.explanation: No article is used before places like school, church, hospital, prison when used for their primary purpose (going to school to study).

questions.question: Choose the correct option: We visited ______ Taj Mahal during our trip to Agra.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' before names of famous buildings, monuments, and historical structures.

questions.question: Choose the correct option: His uncle is ______ heir to a large fortune.
questions.explanation: 'Heir' starts with a silent 'h', so the first sound is the vowel /e/. We use 'an' before silent-h words.
